plant_profile.history: Pomegranate leaves have been utilized in Ayurveda for centuries as an astringent and cooling herb.
plant_profile.physical_characteristics: Small, glossy, lance-shaped opposite leaves growing on a thorny shrub or small tree.
plant_profile.distribution: Native to Iran and northern India, cultivated widely throughout the Mediterranean and tropical regions.

medicinal_uses.use_1: Decoction is used as a mouthwash for oral ulcers and throat infections.
medicinal_uses.use_2: Paste is applied to skin eruptions and to halt minor bleeding.
medicinal_uses.use_3: Juice or extract helps relieve digestive disorders like diarrhea and dysentery.

preparation_methods.method_1: Boil 10-15 leaves in water to make a medicinal decoction.
preparation_methods.method_2: Crush fresh leaves into a fine paste for external application.

dosage.adults: 10-20ml of fresh leaf juice or 20-30ml of decoction.

side_effects: Excessive consumption may cause constipation due to high tannins
drug_interactions: May interact with certain metabolic medications
safety.toxicity_notes: Safe for external and internal use.
